volumeTitle: Children of Heracles. Hippolytus. Andromache. Hecuba
label: Hecuba (Section 722 - Section 840)

sections.label: Section 722
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον , ὥς σε πολυπονωτάτην βροτῶν
sections.translation: Alas for you ! how some deity , whose hand is heavy on you , has sent you troubles beyond all other mortals ! But I see our lord and master

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: Ἀγαμέμνονος , τοὐνθένδε σιγῶμεν , φίλαι .
sections.translation: Agamemnon ; so let us be still from now on , my friends .

sections.label: Section 726
sections.original: Ἑκάβη , τί μέλλεις παῖδα σὴν κρύπτειν τάφῳ
sections.translation: Hecuba , why are you delaying to come and bury your daughter ? for it was for this that Talthybius brought me your message begging that no one of the Argives should touch your child . And so we granted this , and are not touching her ,

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: σὺ δὲ σχολάζεις , ὥστε θαυμάζειν ἐμέ .
sections.translation: but this delay of yours fills me with wonder . And so I have come to send you from here ; for our part there is well performed — if here there is any place for well .

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: ἔα · τίνʼ ἄνδρα τόνδʼ ἐπὶ σκηναῖς ὁρῶ
sections.translation: He sees the body . Oh ! what man is this I see near the tents , some Trojan ’ s corpse ? It is not an Argive ’ s body ;

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: δέμας περιπτύσσοντες ἀγγέλλουσί μοι .
sections.translation: that the garments it is clad in tell me .

sections.label: Section 736
sections.original: δύστηνʼ , ἐμαυτὴν γὰρ λέγω λέγουσα σέ ,
sections.translation: Unhappy one ! in naming you I name myself ; Hecuba , what shall I do ? throw myself here at Agamemnon ’ s knees , or bear my sorrows in silence ?

sections.label: Section 739
sections.original: τί μοι προσώπῳ νῶτον ἐγκλίνασα σὸν
sections.translation: Why do you turn your back towards me and

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: δύρῃ , τὸ πραχθὲν δʼ οὐ λέγεις ; — τίς ἔσθʼ ὅδε ;
sections.translation: weep , refusing to say what has happened ? Who is this ?

sections.label: Section 741
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , εἴ με δούλην πολεμίαν θʼ ἡγούμενος
sections.translation: But if he should count me as a slave and foe and spurn me from his knees , I would add to my anguish .

sections.label: Section 743
sections.original: οὔτοι πέφυκα μάντις , ὥστε μὴ κλύων
sections.translation: I am no prophet born ; therefore , if I am not told , I cannot learn the current of your thoughts .

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἐκλογίζομαί γε πρὸς τὸ δυσμενὲς
sections.translation: Can it be that in estimating this man ’ s feelings I make him out too ill-disposed , when he is not really so ?

sections.label: Section 747
sections.original: εἴ τοί με βούλῃ τῶνδε μηδὲν εἰδέναι ,
sections.translation: If your wish really is that I should remain in ignorance , we are of one mind ; for I have no wish myself to listen .

sections.label: Section 749
sections.original: οὐκ ἂν δυναίμην τοῦδε τιμωρεῖν ἄτερ
sections.translation: Without his aid I shall not be able to avenge

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: τέκνοισι τοῖς ἐμοῖσι . τί στρέφω τάδε ;
sections.translation: my children . Why do I still ponder the matter ? I must do and dare whether I win or lose . Turning to Agamemnon Agamemnon , by your knees , by your beard and conquering hand I implore you —

sections.label: Section 754
sections.original: τί χρῆμα μαστεύουσα ; μῶν ἐλεύθερον
sections.translation: What is your desire ? to be

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: αἰῶνα θέσθαι ; ῥᾴδιον γάρ ἐστί σοι .
sections.translation: set free ? that is easily done .

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: οὐ δῆτα · τοὺς κακοὺς δὲ τιμωρουμένη
sections.translation: Not that ; give me vengeance on the wicked , and I am willing to lead a life of slavery forever .

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: καὶ δὴ τίνʼ ἡμᾶς εἰς ἐπάρκεσιν καλεῖς ;
sections.translation: Well , but why do you call me to your aid ?

sections.label: Section 759
sections.original: οὐδέν τι τούτων ὧν σὺ δοξάζεις , ἄναξ . —
sections.translation: It is a matter you little know of , king .

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: ὁρᾷς νεκρὸν τόνδʼ , οὗ καταστάζω δάκρυ ;
sections.translation: Do you see this corpse , for whom my tears now flow ?

sections.label: Section 761
sections.original: ὁρῶ · τὸ μέντοι μέλλον οὐκ ἔχω μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: I do ; but what is to follow , I cannot guess .

sections.label: Section 762
sections.original: τοῦτόν ποτʼ ἔτεκον κἄφερον ζώνης ὕπο .
sections.translation: He was once my child ; I bore him in my womb .

sections.label: Section 763
sections.original: ἔστιν δὲ τίς σῶν οὗτος , ὦ τλῆμον , τέκνων ;
sections.translation: Which of your sons is he , poor sufferer ?

sections.label: Section 764
sections.original: οὐ τῶν θανόντων Πριαμιδῶν ὑπʼ Ἰλίῳ .
sections.translation: Not one of Priam ’ s race who fell beneath Ilium ’ s walls .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: ἦ γάρ τινʼ ἄλλον ἔτεκες ἢ κείνους , γύναι ;
sections.translation: Did you indeed have another son besides those , lady ?

sections.label: Section 766
sections.original: ἀνόνητά γʼ , ὡς ἔοικε , τόνδʼ ὃν εἰσορᾷς .
sections.translation: Yes , the one you see here , of whom it seems I have small gain .

sections.label: Section 767
sections.original: ποῦ δʼ ὢν ἐτύγχανʼ , ἡνίκʼ ὤλλυτο πτόλις ;
sections.translation: Where then was he , when his city was being destroyed ?

sections.label: Section 768
sections.original: πατήρ νιν ἐξέπεμψεν ὀρρωδῶν θανεῖν .
sections.translation: His father , fearful of his death , conveyed him out of Troy .

sections.label: Section 769
sections.original: ποῖ τῶν τότʼ ὄντων χωρίσας τέκνων μόνον ;
sections.translation: Where did he place him apart from the sons he then had ?

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: ἐς τήνδε χώραν , οὗπερ ηὑρέθη θανών .
sections.translation: In this very land , where his corpse was found .

sections.label: Section 771
sections.original: πρὸς ἄνδρʼ ὃς ἄρχει τῆσδε Πολυμήστωρ χθονός ;
sections.translation: With Polymestor , the king of this country ?

sections.label: Section 772
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ἐπέμφθη πικροτάτου χρυσοῦ φύλαξ .
sections.translation: He was sent here in charge of gold , most bitter trust !

sections.label: Section 773
sections.original: θνῄσκει δὲ πρὸς τοῦ καὶ τίνος πότμου τυχών ;
sections.translation: By whom was he slain ? What death overtook him ?

sections.label: Section 774
sections.original: τίνος γʼ ὑπʼ ἄλλου ; Θρῄξ νιν ὤλεσε ξένος .
sections.translation: By whom but this man ? His Thracian host slew him .

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ὦ τλῆμον · ἦ που χρυσὸν ἠράσθη λαβεῖν ;
sections.translation: The wretch ! could he have been so eager for the gold ?

sections.label: Section 776
sections.original: τοιαῦτʼ , ἐπειδὴ συμφορὰν ἔγνω Φρυγῶν .
sections.translation: Just so , when he heard of the Phrygians ’ disaster .

sections.label: Section 777
sections.original: ηὗρες δὲ ποῦ νιν ; ἢ τίς ἤνεγκεν νεκρόν ;
sections.translation: Where did you find him ? or did some one bring his corpse ?

sections.label: Section 778
sections.original: ἥδʼ , ἐντυχοῦσα ποντίας ἀκτῆς ἔπι .
sections.translation: This woman , who happened upon it on the sea-shore .

sections.label: Section 779
sections.original: τοῦτον ματεύουσʼ ἢ πονοῦσʼ ἄλλον πόνον ;
sections.translation: Was she seeking it , or bent on other tasks ?

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: λούτρʼ ᾤχετʼ οἴσουσʼ ἐξ ἁλὸς Πολυξένῃ .
sections.translation: She had gone to fetch water from the sea to wash Polyxena .

sections.label: Section 781
sections.original: κτανών νιν , ὡς ἔοικεν , ἐκβάλλει ξένος .
sections.translation: It seems then his host slew him and cast his body out to sea .

sections.label: Section 782
sections.original: θαλασσόπλαγκτόν γʼ , ὧδε διατεμὼν χρόα .
sections.translation: Yes , for the waves to toss , after mangling him in this way .

sections.label: Section 783
sections.original: ὦ σχετλία σὺ τῶν ἀμετρήτων πόνων .
sections.translation: Woe to you for your measureless troubles !

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: ὄλωλα κοὐδὲν λοιπόν , Ἀγάμεμνον , κακῶν .
sections.translation: I am ruined ; no evil now is left , Agamemnon .

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · τίς οὕτω δυστυχὴς ἔφυ γυνή ;
sections.translation: Ah ! what woman was ever born to such mischance ?

sections.label: Section 786
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν , εἰ μὴ τὴν Τύχην αὐτὴν λέγοις .
sections.translation: There is no one , unless you would name Chance herself . But hear my reason for throwing myself at your knees . If my treatment seems to you deserved , I will be content ; but , if otherwise , help me to punish

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: τιμωρὸς ἀνδρός , ἀνοσιωτάτου ξένου ,
sections.translation: this most godless host , fearless alike of gods in heaven or hell , who has done a most unholy deed ; who , though often he had shared my board and been counted first of all my guest-friends

sections.label: Section 795
sections.original: τυχὼν δʼ ὅσων δεῖ — . καὶ λαβὼν προμηθίαν
sections.translation: meeting with every kindness he could claim —. And receiving my consideration , he slew my son , and bent though he was on murder , did not think it right to bury him , but cast his body forth to sea .

sections.label: Section 798
sections.original: ἡμεῖς μὲν οὖν δοῦλοί τε κἀσθενεῖς ἴσως ·
sections.translation: I may be a slave and weak as well , but the gods are strong , and Custom too which prevails over them ,

sections.label: Section 800
sections.original: Νόμος · νόμῳ γὰρ τοὺς θεοὺς ἡγούμεθα
sections.translation: for by custom it is that we believe in them and set up boundaries of right and wrong for our lives . Now if this principle , when referred to you , is to be set at nothing , and they are to escape punishment who murder guests or dare to plunder the temples of gods ,

sections.label: Section 805
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστιν οὐδὲν τῶν ἐν ἀνθρώποις ἴσον .
sections.translation: then all fairness in human matters is at an end . Consider this then a disgrace and show regard for me , have pity on me , and , like an artist standing back from his picture , look on me and closely scan my piteous state . I was once a queen , but now I am your slave ;

sections.label: Section 810
sections.original: εὔπαις ποτʼ οὖσα , νῦν δὲ γραῦς ἄπαις θʼ ἅμα ,
sections.translation: a happy mother once , but now childless and old alike , bereft of city , utterly forlorn , the most wretched woman living .

sections.label: Section 812
sections.original: οἴμοι τάλαινα , ποῖ μʼ ὑπεξάγεις πόδα ;
sections.translation: as Agamemnon is turning away . Ah ! woe is me ! where would you withdraw your steps from me ? My efforts then will be in vain , ah me ! Why , oh ! why do we mortals toil , as we must , and seek out all other sciences ,

sections.label: Section 815
sections.original: μοχθοῦμεν ὡς χρὴ πάντα καὶ ματεύομεν ,
sections.translation: but Persuasion , the only real mistress of mankind , we take no further pains to master completely by offering to pay for the knowledge , so that any man could convince his fellows as he pleased and gain his point at once ?

sections.label: Section 820
sections.original: πῶς οὖν ἔτʼ ἄν τις ἐλπίσαι πράξειν καλῶς ;
sections.translation: How shall anyone hereafter hope for prosperity ? All those my sons are gone from me , and she , my daughter , is a slave and suffers shame . I am lost ; I see the smoke leaping over my city . Further — though this is perhaps idly urged ,

sections.label: Section 825
sections.original: Κύπριν προβάλλειν · ἀλλʼ ὅμως εἰρήσεται ·
sections.translation: to plead your love , still I will put the case — at your side lies my daughter , Cassandra , the inspired maiden , as the Phrygians call her . How then , king , will you acknowledge those nights of rapture , or what return shall my daughter or I her mother have

sections.label: Section 830
sections.original: χάριν τίνʼ ἕξει παῖς ἐμή , κείνης δʼ ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: for the love she has lavished on her lord ? For from darkness and the endearments of the night mortals have their keenest joys . Listen , then ; do you see this corpse ? By doing him a service , you will do it to a kinsman of your bride ’ s .

sections.label: Section 835
sections.original: δράσεις . ἑνός μοι μῦθος ἐνδεὴς ἔτι .
sections.translation: I have only one thing yet to urge . Oh ! would I had a voice in arms , in hands , in hair and feet , placed there by the arts of Daedalus or some god , that all together they might with tears embrace your knees ,

sections.label: Section 840
sections.original: κλαίοντʼ , ἐπισκήπτοντα παντοίους λόγους .
sections.translation: bringing a thousand pleas to bear on you ! O my lord and master , most glorious light of Hellas , listen , stretch forth a helping hand to this aged woman , for all she is a thing of nothing ; still do so . For it is always a good man ’ s duty to help the right ,
